event1.text: You saw the Incident Alpha report.

event8.text: Most archive sectors were corrupted during the blackout.

event13.text: Or at least they should be.

event1.title: ARCHIVE MAP
event1.content:
Recovered maintenance map of the station archive sector.

Sector A: Primary relay archive
Sector B: Corrupted personnel cache
Sector C: Emergency channel records

event3.text: Sector C. Emergency channel records. That shouldn't still be indexed.

event1.text: There may still be something left inside. If the archive survived, answers survived. That's the comforting version. The less comforting version is that someone wanted it to survive.

event3.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event3.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Status: Locked

event1.title: RECORDING TRANSCRIPT
event1.content:
21:14 Door 12 opened.
21:15 Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 Security override accepted.
21:19 Signal interruption.

event6.text: That recording should not exist. And before you ask, yes, I know how terrible that sounds.

event1.text: Because I deleted that archive myself. Or I thought I did.
Some records were never meant to survive. And I am beginning to think this one survived on purpose.

event3.text: That figure... the angle is wrong, but the suit outline is familiar. I hate when evidence has better memory than I do.

event1.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event1.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN

event3.text: Authorization valid. Source unknown. That's not how security logs work.

event0.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event0.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

event2.text: 7.14 MHz. No, no, no. We are not touching that channel.

event1.text: Listen to me. Whatever happens next... Do not open channel 7.14. I don't care what it promises you.
Because someone else is listening. And if it answers... don't answer back.
Good. Then don't make the same mistake we did. We thought backup channels were safer. Turns out safety can be very loud when it's lying.
